questionText: Which sentence correctly uses the comparative form of an adjective?
options: This exercise is more easy than the previous one.
This exercise is easier than the previous one.
This exercise is more easier than the previous one.
This exercise is easy than the previous one.
explanation: Pour les adjectifs courts (une ou deux syllabes se terminant par -y, -er, -ow), on ajoute '-er'. 'Easy' se transforme en 'easier'. 'More easy' est incorrect, et 'more easier' est un double comparatif.

questionText: Complete the sentence: 'This is _______ book I have ever read.'
options: the most interesting
the more interesting
most interesting
more interesting
explanation: Le superlatif ('the most + adjectif long') est utilisé pour comparer un élément à tous les autres dans un groupe. 'I have ever read' indique qu'on parle de tous les livres lus. 'More interesting' est un comparatif.

options: My car is as fast like your car.
My car is so fast as your car.
My car is as fast as your car.
My car is faster as your car.
explanation: Pour exprimer l'égalité, on utilise la structure 'as + adjectif + as'. 'As fast as' est correct. 'Like' et 'so' ne sont pas utilisés dans ce contexte, et 'faster as' est incorrect.

questionText: Choose the correct irregular comparative: 'The situation is getting _______ and worse.'
options: bad
more bad
worse
worst
explanation: 'Bad' est un adjectif irrégulier. Son comparatif est 'worse'. 'Worst' est le superlatif. La structure 'comparatif and comparatif' (worse and worse) montre une progression.

questionText: Which sentence uses the superlative form correctly?
options: She is the more intelligent student in the class.
She is the intelligentest student in the class.
She is the most intelligent student in the class.
She is most intelligent student in the class.
explanation: Pour les adjectifs longs (trois syllabes ou plus comme 'intelligent'), on utilise 'the most' pour former le superlatif. 'Intelligentest' n'existe pas, et 'the more' est un comparatif.

questionText: Based on the image, complete the sentence: 'The increase in sales for Product A is _______ the decrease for Product B.'
options: as dramatic as
more dramatic than
the most dramatic
less dramatic than
explanation: Le graphique montre une hausse spectaculaire pour le produit A et une légère baisse pour le produit B. On compare donc les deux: la hausse est PLUS spectaculaire QUE la baisse. 'More dramatic than' est le comparatif correct.

questionText: Identify the mistake in this sentence: 'Of all the students in the class, Paul is the taller.'
options: Of all the students
in the class
the taller
Paul is
explanation: Quand on compare plus de deux éléments ('Of all the students'), on doit utiliser le superlatif, pas le comparatif. La forme correcte est 'the tallest'. 'Taller' sert à comparer deux personnes seulement.

questionText: Complete this proverb: 'The _______ you go, the harder the return.'
options: far
farthest
more far
farther
explanation: Il s'agit d'une structure corrélative 'The + comparatif, the + comparatif' (plus... plus...). 'Far' a deux comparatifs: 'farther' (distance physique) et 'further' (distance figurative). Ici, 'farther' convient pour une distance physique.

questionText: Which option correctly transforms 'My house is big. Your house is big too.' into a single sentence using 'as...as'?
options: My house is as big as your house.
Your house is as bigger as my house.
My house is so big as your house.
Your house is not as big as my house.
explanation: 'As...as' exprime l'égalité. La phrase originale dit que les deux maisons sont grandes, donc elles sont égales. 'My house is as big as your house' est correct. 'Not as big as' exprimerait une inégalité.

questionText: Choose the sentence that uses 'less' correctly to form an inferiority comparison.
options: This exercise is less harder than the previous one.
This exercise is less hard than the previous one.
This exercise is less hardest than the previous one.
This exercise is less more hard than the previous one.
explanation: Pour exprimer l'infériorité avec des adjectifs longs ou courts, on utilise 'less + adjectif + than'. 'Less hard' est correct. On ne combine pas 'less' avec un autre comparatif (-er, more).
